name: Number of unique subjects taught by each teacher
User activity for the past 30 days i
1. List all customers and their orders. Include customers with no orders.
hint: Use LEFT JOIN and then use UNION with a subquery

name: 2. Find orders placed by customers from the USA.
hint: Use INNER JOIN or subquery with IN

name: 3. Get a list of all customers and products, even if they aren’t related.
hint: Use CROSS JOIN

name: 4. List all employees and their manager names.
hint: Use SELF JOIN on Employees. Output includes manager name column using manager_id.

name: 5. List all orders and include customer names, even if the customer is unknown.
hint: Use RIGHT JOIN (rare), or FULL OUTER JOIN

name: 6. Find customers who placed at least one order.
hint: Use INNER JOIN or EXISTS

name: 7. Show all customers who haven’t ordered any products.
hint: Use LEFT JOIN + IS NULL or NOT EXISTS

name: 8. List products and the total quantity ordered, including products never ordered.
hint: Use LEFT JOIN from products to order_items

name: 9. Show pairs of employees who belong to the same department.
hint: Use SELF JOIN with a WHERE e1.emp_id != e2.emp_id

name: 10. List customer-product pairs for every order placed.
hint: Join Orders, Order_Items, Products

name: 11. List all names from customers and employees (no duplicates).
hint: Use UNION

name: 12. List all names from customers and employees (with duplicates).
hint: Use UNION ALL

name: 13. Find all IDs that appear in both Customers and Employees.
hint: Use INTERSECT (if supported) or IN subquery

name: 14. List all orders and employees by their ID and name in a combined view.
hint: Use UNION ALL + aliases

name: 15. Return only the unique customer countries and employee departments.
hint: Use UNION with alias as "Region"

name: 16. Get a combined list of customer and product names.
hint: Use UNION between Customers and Products

name: 17. Find customers who have placed more than 1 order.
hint: Use correlated subquery or group by + having

name: 18. List orders with amount above the average.
hint: Use scalar subquery

name: 19. Get customers who ordered a Phone.
hint: Use subquery or JOIN with filter on product_name

name: 20. Return products that were never ordered.
hint: Use NOT EXISTS or LEFT JOIN + IS NULL

name: 21. List customers who ordered all product categories.
hint: Use subquery with GROUP BY and comparison

name: 22. Find orders with the maximum amount.
hint: Scalar subquery or window function

name: 23. Find customers whose average order amount is greater than the average order amount of all the orders
24. List customers who placed orders only in January.
hint: Correlated subquery with NOT EXISTS for other months

name: 25. Find top 1 recent order per product.
hint: Correlated subquery or ROW_NUMBER()

name: 26. Find customers whose total order amount > the average of the total order amounts of all customers
hint: Use subquery for avg

name: 27. List customers with their latest order date and total amount spent.
hint: Use subquery or JOIN + GROUP BY

name: 28. Show all combinations of customer and their ordered products.
hint: Multiple JOINs or subquery

name: 29. List products bought in the most recent order.
hint: Use subquery for latest date, then JOIN

name: 30. Find employees who are not managing anyone.
hint: Subquery with NOT IN (SELECT manager_id)
